speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: Powell said the Committee would keep policy restrictive and remain attentive to both sides of its dual mandate while inflation continues toward 2%.

speeches.name: Lisa D. Cook
speeches.summary: Cook emphasized the need to balance inflation progress with labor-market resilience, signaling a cautious but still restrictive stance.

speeches.name: Michelle W. Bowman
speeches.role: Vice Chair for Supervision, Federal Reserve Board
speeches.summary: Bowman stressed that policy should remain sufficiently restrictive until inflation is convincingly on track to 2%.

speeches.name: Lorie K. Logan
speeches.role: President, Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas
speeches.summary: Logan highlighted the importance of keeping financial conditions tight enough to finish the inflation fight without unnecessary labor-market damage.

speeches.name: Austan Goolsbee
speeches.role: President, Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago
speeches.summary: Goolsbee noted that disinflation has made progress but argued the Fed should wait for clearer evidence before easing further.

macro_fred.narrative: Leaning Neutral: CPI at 4.3%, Fed Funds at 3.62%, unemployment at 4.3%.
